intro: Odixia angusta, also known as Australian desert saltbush, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its compact growth habit and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the severity of respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Odixia angusta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and skin conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
